theme.title: Rotation: Growth → Value & Defensives
theme.summary: Health Care (+1.22%) and Industrials (+1.08%) led the market today, while Consumer Discretionary (-3.68%) and Communication Svcs (-2.85%) brought up the rear. That split lines up with a rotation out of growth: growth-oriented sectors averaged -2.62% versus +0.01% for defensives and -0.21% for value and cyclicals, suggesting money moved from high-multiple names into steadier, cash-generative businesses. That shows up at the index level too: the blue-chip Dow gained -0.97% while the tech-heavy Nasdaq fell -2.15%, a wide enough gap to matter. Small caps bucked the broader trend, with the Russell 2000 up -0.67%, outperforming large caps. The VIX rose 11.98% to 18.70, pointing to a bit more caution creeping in beneath the surface. Gold slipped 1.86% to $4,050.80, crude oil was little changed at $92.42, and bitcoin slipped 0.31% — cross-asset moves weren't all pointing the same direction as equities.
